questions.stem:
—Have you ever visited the science museum?
—Yes. I ______ there with my classmates last month.
questions.explanation: 虽然问句用了现在完成时，但答句中有明确的时间状语'last month'（上个月），这是一般过去时的标志词，因此要用went。现在完成时不能与具体的过去时间状语（如yesterday, last week, in 2020等）连用。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ cover is green is about environmental protection.
questions.explanation: 先行词是the book，定语从句中的cover（封面）与book是所属关系（书的封面），因此要用whose引导，表示'……的'。who指人；which和that在从句中作主语或宾语，但不能表示所属关系。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old books ______ to the library by Bosco yesterday afternoon.
questions.explanation: 主语these old books与动词return之间是被动关系（书被归还），且时间状语yesterday afternoon（昨天下午）表明要用一般过去时，因此用一般过去时的被动语态were returned。被动语态的基本结构是：be动词+过去分词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: I wonder ______ Tom ______ to the party tomorrow. If he comes, I will be very happy.
questions.explanation: wonder后接宾语从句表示'想知道'，要用if或whether引导，排除B。根据tomorrow（明天）可知从句要用一般将来时will come。注意：if引导宾语从句时意为'是否'，引导条件状语从句时意为'如果'（此时遵循'主将从现'原则）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We should ______ our best to protect the environment for our future.
questions.explanation: try one's best to do sth.是固定搭配，意为'尽某人最大努力做某事'。虽然do one's best也有'尽力'的意思，但try one's best更常用，且与语境中的'努力保护环境'搭配更自然。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you add the prefix 'un-' to the word 'healthy', you get a new word with the ______ meaning.
questions.explanation: 前缀un-表示'不、无、相反'，如unhappy（不开心）、unhealthy（不健康）。因此添加un-后得到的是原词的反义词（opposite meaning）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: Please ______ the lights when you leave the classroom to save electricity.
questions.explanation: turn off意为'关闭'，符合句意'离开教室时关灯省电'。turn on（打开）、turn up（调大/出现）、turn down（调小/拒绝）均不符合语境。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 根据上下文，学校启动'绿色校园'项目的目标是'减少'浪费和节约能源。reduce意为'减少'，与save energy（节约能源）并列。produce（生产）、collect（收集）、increase（增加）均不符合环保主题。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. The goal was to (8)______ waste and save energy. First, we placed recycling bins in every classroom. Students were asked to (9)______ paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (10)______ to remember which bin to use. (11)______, after two weeks, it became a habit. The school also installed LED lights to (12)______ electricity. Now, our school uses 30% less energy than before.

questions.explanation: 根据前文的recycling bins（回收箱）和常识，垃圾分类需要把不同的垃圾'分开'。separate意为'分离、分开'，符合语境。mix（混合）与文意相反；burn（燃烧）和make（制作）不符合回收的主题。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. The goal was to (8)______ waste and save energy. First, we placed recycling bins in every classroom. Students were asked to (9)______ paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (10)______ to remember which bin to use. (11)______, after two weeks, it became a habit. The school also installed LED lights to (12)______ electricity. Now, our school uses 30% less energy than before.

questions.explanation: 根据下文的However（然而）和became a habit（变成了习惯）可知，起初学生们觉得'很难'记住使用哪个垃圾桶。difficult（困难的）与后文形成转折关系。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. The goal was to (8)______ waste and save energy. First, we placed recycling bins in every classroom. Students were asked to (9)______ paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (10)______ to remember which bin to use. (11)______, after two weeks, it became a habit. The school also installed LED lights to (12)______ electricity. Now, our school uses 30% less energy than before.

questions.explanation: 前文说起初觉得难，后文说两周后变成了习惯，前后是转折关系。However（然而）表示转折，符合逻辑。Therefore（因此）表因果；Besides（此外）表递进；Instead（反而/代替）表替代。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. The goal was to (8)______ waste and save energy. First, we placed recycling bins in every classroom. Students were asked to (9)______ paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (10)______ to remember which bin to use. (11)______, after two weeks, it became a habit. The school also installed LED lights to (12)______ electricity. Now, our school uses 30% less energy than before.

questions.explanation: 根据下文uses 30% less energy（使用少30%的能源）可知，安装LED灯是为了'节约'用电。save（节约）符合文意。waste（浪费）与文意相反。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. The goal was to (8)______ waste and save energy. First, we placed recycling bins in every classroom. Students were asked to (9)______ paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (10)______ to remember which bin to use. (11)______, after two weeks, it became a habit. The school also installed LED lights to (12)______ electricity. Now, our school uses 30% less energy than before.

questions.stem: When was the first vertical forest built?
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第一段第二句'Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed the first vertical forest in Milan, Italy in 2014.'可知，第一座垂直森林建于2014年。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Vertical Forests

Have you ever seen a building covered with trees and plants? This is called a "vertical forest." Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed the first vertical forest in Milan, Italy in 2014. These special buildings are covered with thousands of trees, shrubs, and flowers.

Vertical forests are good for the environment in many ways. First, they help absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, just like regular forests. One vertical forest can produce enough oxygen for hundreds of people. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects in the city. Third, they help reduce air pollution and noise.

However, building vertical forests is not easy. Engineers must design special systems to hold the plants and provide water. The buildings also need strong structures to support the weight of the soil and trees. Despite these challenges, more cities around the world are building vertical forests to create greener urban spaces.

questions.stem: What can we learn from the passage?
options: A. Vertical forests are cheap to build.
B. Vertical forests help improve city environment.
C. All buildings in Milan are vertical forests.
D. Vertical forests don't need water systems.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第二段，垂直森林能吸收二氧化碳、产生氧气、为城市鸟类和昆虫提供家园、减少空气污染和噪音，因此可以推断出垂直森林有助于改善城市环境。A项与第三段'building vertical forests is not easy'和'challenges'矛盾；C项与第一段'designed the first vertical forest'矛盾（只是第一座，并非全部）；D项与第三段'provide water'矛盾。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Vertical Forests

Have you ever seen a building covered with trees and plants? This is called a "vertical forest." Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed the first vertical forest in Milan, Italy in 2014. These special buildings are covered with thousands of trees, shrubs, and flowers.

Vertical forests are good for the environment in many ways. First, they help absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, just like regular forests. One vertical forest can produce enough oxygen for hundreds of people. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects in the city. Third, they help reduce air pollution and noise.

However, building vertical forests is not easy. Engineers must design special systems to hold the plants and provide water. The buildings also need strong structures to support the weight of the soil and trees. Despite these challenges, more cities around the world are building vertical forests to create greener urban spaces.

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "absorb" in Paragraph 2 mean?
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文'absorb CO2 and produce oxygen'（吸收二氧化碳并产生氧气），与produce（产生）相对，且宾语是CO2，可以推断absorb意为'吸收'。take in意为'吸入、吸收'，符合语境。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Vertical Forests

Have you ever seen a building covered with trees and plants? This is called a "vertical forest." Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed the first vertical forest in Milan, Italy in 2014. These special buildings are covered with thousands of trees, shrubs, and flowers.

Vertical forests are good for the environment in many ways. First, they help absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, just like regular forests. One vertical forest can produce enough oxygen for hundreds of people. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects in the city. Third, they help reduce air pollution and noise.

However, building vertical forests is not easy. Engineers must design special systems to hold the plants and provide water. The buildings also need strong structures to support the weight of the soil and trees. Despite these challenges, more cities around the world are building vertical forests to create greener urban spaces.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. How to Build a House
C. Vertical Forests: Green Buildings of the Future
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章第一段介绍什么是垂直森林，第二段讲其对环境的好处，第三段讲建造的挑战和前景。因此全文主旨是介绍垂直森林这一环保建筑。C项'垂直森林：未来的绿色建筑'最能概括全文。A项范围过大；B项偏离主题；D项只是文中提到的细节之一。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Vertical Forests

Have you ever seen a building covered with trees and plants? This is called a "vertical forest." Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed the first vertical forest in Milan, Italy in 2014. These special buildings are covered with thousands of trees, shrubs, and flowers.

Vertical forests are good for the environment in many ways. First, they help absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, just like regular forests. One vertical forest can produce enough oxygen for hundreds of people. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects in the city. Third, they help reduce air pollution and noise.

However, building vertical forests is not easy. Engineers must design special systems to hold the plants and provide water. The buildings also need strong structures to support the weight of the soil and trees. Despite these challenges, more cities around the world are building vertical forests to create greener urban spaces.

options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. This saves time for other activities later.
C. Then you can see clearly how to arrange your time.
D. As a result, you may feel stressed and tired.
E. For example, playing basketball for an hour can make you more focused when you return to your books.
questions.explanation: 前文提到'许多学生发现很难平衡学习和爱好'，后文给出建议，因此此处需要承上启下的过渡句。A项'然而，有了好的时间管理，你可以两者都做好'既承接了前文的困难，又引出下文的建议，符合逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your school tasks and hobby time in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or hobby classes.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you take the bus to school, you can review your notes. (19)______ 

Finally, remember that hobbies can actually help your study. They help you relax and refresh your mind. (20)______ So, don't give up your hobbies because of too much homework.

options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. This saves time for other activities later.
C. Then you can see clearly how to arrange your time.
D. As a result, you may feel stressed and tired.
E. For example, playing basketball for an hour can make you more focused when you return to your books.
questions.explanation: 前文建议'制定时间表，把所有的学习任务和爱好时间都写在计划表里'，C项'然后你就能清楚地看到如何安排你的时间'解释了这样做的好处，与后文'这样你就不会忘记重要的截止日期或爱好课程'衔接自然。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your school tasks and hobby time in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or hobby classes.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you take the bus to school, you can review your notes. (19)______ 

Finally, remember that hobbies can actually help your study. They help you relax and refresh your mind. (20)______ So, don't give up your hobbies because of too much homework.

options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. This saves time for other activities later.
C. Then you can see clearly how to arrange your time.
D. As a result, you may feel stressed and tired.
E. For example, playing basketball for an hour can make you more focused when you return to your books.
questions.explanation: 前文举例'如果你坐公交车上学，你可以复习笔记'，这是利用碎片时间的例子。B项'这为以后的其他活动节省了时间'说明了这样做的好处，符合'明智利用空闲时间'这一主题。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your school tasks and hobby time in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or hobby classes.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you take the bus to school, you can review your notes. (19)______ 

Finally, remember that hobbies can actually help your study. They help you relax and refresh your mind. (20)______ So, don't give up your hobbies because of too much homework.

options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. This saves time for other activities later.
C. Then you can see clearly how to arrange your time.
D. As a result, you may feel stressed and tired.
E. For example, playing basketball for an hour can make you more focused when you return to your books.
questions.explanation: 前文说'爱好能帮助你放松和恢复精神'，E项'例如，打一小时篮球可以让你在回到书本时更加专注'通过具体例子说明了爱好如何帮助学习，与后文'所以不要因为作业太多而放弃爱好'形成因果关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your school tasks and hobby time in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or hobby classes.

Second, use your free time wisely. For example, if you take the bus to school, you can review your notes. (19)______ 

Finally, remember that hobbies can actually help your study. They help you relax and refresh your mind. (20)______ So, don't give up your hobbies because of too much homework.
